name: Personalised First-Line Outreach
summary: An outbound technique that uses the first line of a message to prove relevance quickly and earn the buyer's attention.
whenToUseIt: Use personalised first-line outreach in cold email or LinkedIn when one sharp, relevant opening observation can make the rest of the message easier to trust.
steps: Choose one relevant fact, trigger, or public priority connected to the buyer's world.
Link it to a business hypothesis rather than leaving it as trivia.
Use the rest of the message to explore that hypothesis briefly.
example: Instead of commenting on a hobby or school, the first line references the buyer's public note about ramping a new team and connects it to coaching consistency risk.
